FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class EditableText

Namespace
FluentGpu.Controls
Assembly
FluentGpu.Controls.dll

The WinUI-parity text editor every text control composes (TextBox/PasswordBox/NumberBox/AutoSuggestBox/editable ComboBox/ColorPicker), built on TextEditCore (document + caret/selection/undo) and the IFontSystem editor queries (hit-test / caret metrics / range rects — the SAME layout pipeline the renderer draws with).

Document ↔ signal. The caller-visible Signal<T> stays the single source of truth: a user edit mutates the core document and then makes ONE text.Value write (TextBind updates just the text node — no component re-render per keystroke); an external/programmatic signal write is folded back into the document by the TextBind evaluation (core.ResetText, reason = ProgrammaticChange). An IME composition is the one sanctioned divergence: the PROVISIONAL text lives in the document (so it renders, with clause underlines) but the signal updates only on commit.

Rendering. Caret/selection/IME decorations are retained scene state (TextEditState + the pooled rect slab on the editor's TEXT node), replayed by the recorder — never composed elements. All geometry this control writes (caret X, selection/underline rects) is in RAW text-node-local (document-space) coordinates; the caret-follow -ScrollX is applied ONCE, as the text node's wrapper transform (a TransformBind reading a FloatSignal — the compositor-bypass idiom), so the recorder's world transform shifts glyphs and decorations together. ScrollX mirrors the applied offset for tests/IME math.

Focus. Focus state is engine-driven (OnFocusChanged — WinUI GotFocus/LostFocus): gained → arm the caret blinker + IME (sink + editable) + capture the Escape-revert snapshot; keyboard (Tab) focus additionally selects all (pointer focus keeps the press-placed caret). Lost → disarm blinker/IME; the selection STATE is kept but its visuals hide (SelectionActive off). Enter fires OnCommit and KEEPS focus (WinUI); Escape reverts to the focus-time snapshot, fires OnCancel, and blurs (the established engine contract that NumberBox/ComboBox/ColorPicker rely on).

Deviations (documented, not hidden): BeforeTextChanging gates INSERTIONS (typing, paste, IME commit, newline); deletions and undo/redo are not cancelable (WinUI fires it for every change). A Sanitize that rewrites the value replaces the whole document as one undo step with the caret at the end. Drag after a double-click extends by character, not by word. IME clause underlines sit at the line bottom (face-metric underline placement arrives with the DirectWrite decoration work).

Focused border. WinUI's Focused state swaps BorderBrush to TextControlElevationBorderFocusedBrush (the accent-bottom-stop gradient) with BorderThickness 1,1,1,2 (TextBox_themeresources.xaml, the Focused VisualState ~lines 295–304 + the brush definition ~48–66). Visually that is a neutral 1px ring whose BOTTOM edge reads as a 2px accent bar. We keep the neutral Tok.ControlElevationBorder ring in every state and draw the separate 2px accent bottom bar while focused — the same pixels without double-drawing an accent gradient under an accent bar.

public sealed class EditableText : Component

Fields

AcceptsReturn

Multi-line: Enter inserts '\r' (Ctrl+Enter commits), Up/Down/Page navigate lines, text wraps.

public bool AcceptsReturn

Field Value

bool

BeforeTextChanging

Cancelable insert gate (WinUI BeforeTextChanging): receives the PROPOSED full text; return false to reject. Gates typing/paste/IME-commit/newline (deletions and undo are not cancelable here).

public Func<string, bool>? BeforeTextChanging

Field Value

Func<string, bool>

CaretColor 

public ColorF CaretColor

Field Value

ColorF

Chromeless

Chromeless composition mode — the WinUI editable-ComboBox TextBox part. In that template ONE Background border spans both grid columns (ComboBox_themeresources.xaml:571, Grid.ColumnSpan="2") and the TextBox part mounts INSIDE it with BorderBrush="Transparent" + Style=ComboBoxTextBoxStyle (:580, :770–813). True ⇒ this control paints NO chrome of its own: no resting fill, no 1px elevation border, no corner rounding, and no focused 2px accent bottom bar (the COMPOSER's outer box owns all of those). Caret/selection/IME/padding and focus behavior stay intact, and the part keeps ONLY the WinUI TextControl interaction fills it paints inside the shared border (ComboBoxTextBoxStyle PointerOver/Focused, ComboBox_themeresources.xaml:786–803): hover = TextControlBackgroundPointerOver = ControlFillColorSecondary (TextBox_themeresources.xaml:24/:131), focused = TextControlBackgroundFocused = ControlFillColorInputActive (:25/:132). Default false — TextBox/PasswordBox/ NumberBox/AutoSuggestBox keep their full chrome.

public bool Chromeless

Field Value

bool

OnTextChanged

Fired AFTER the signal write on every text change (user or programmatic); read LastChangeReason for the WinUI change reason.

public Action<string>? OnTextChanged

Field Value

Action<string>

PartDeleteButton

The ✕ clear button (WinUI DeleteButton), mounted while focused ∧ non-empty. Owned: OnClick (the clear-keep-focus mechanics), TabStop (false — pointer focus must resolve to the field), Role.

public const string PartDeleteButton = "DeleteButton"

Field Value

string

PartLane

The padded, clipping content viewport (WinUI ContentElement). Owned: ClipToBounds (the caret-follow crop), Children (the scroller wrapper carrying the −ScrollX TransformBind + the caret/IME text node), OnRealized (chained). Padding IS stylable here — the public path the internal LanePadding composer seam maps to.

public const string PartLane = "Lane"

Field Value

string

PartRoot

The focusable field-chrome root (WinUI BorderElement). Owned: OnFocusChanged/OnKeyDown/OnCharInput/ OnPointerPressed/OnDrag (the edit mechanics), IsEnabled, Focusable, Role, ZStack, Children, OnRealized (chained). The state fills/border/corners are recomputed per render BEFORE the modifier — restyle freely.

public const string PartRoot = "Root"

Field Value

string

PartText

The visible text run (a TextEl — use Parts.Set<TextEl>(PartText, …)). Owned: TextBind/ ColorBind (the document/placeholder display), Wrap + Width (the AcceptsReturn wrap mechanics).

public const string PartText = "Text"

Field Value

string

Parts

Lightweight per-part styling (CSS ::part): modifiers keyed by the PartXxx consts; see TemplateParts for the contract.

public TemplateParts? Parts

Field Value

TemplateParts

Placeholder 

public string Placeholder

Field Value

string

Revealed

PasswordBox Peek/Visible: show the REAL text while keeping the Mask password semantics (copy/cut stay blocked — WinUI never allows copying out of a PasswordBox, revealed or not).

public bool Revealed

Field Value

bool

RightAffix

Optional right-edge affix (PasswordBox reveal "eye", NumberBox spin column). Laid out at the right of the field, stretched to the inner height; the text area grows to fill the rest. When set, it REPLACES the WinUI DeleteButton lane (in WinUI the spin/reveal buttons take the affix slot instead of the delete button). Composers that mount/unmount the affix on a LIVE instance must go through SetRightAffix(Element) — a bare field write does not re-render the field.

public Element? RightAffix

Field Value

Element

Sanitize 

public Func<string, string>? Sanitize

Field Value

Func<string, string>

ShowDeleteButton

Show the WinUI TextBox DeleteButton (✕, glyph E894) while focused ∧ non-empty (single-line, no Mask, not read-only, and only when no RightAffix occupies the lane). TextBox passes true.

public bool ShowDeleteButton

Field Value

bool

Properties

Core

The editing state machine — the honest selection/caret/undo surface (SelectionStart, CanUndo, UndoDepth…). Reads are free; for programmatic mutations prefer the wrappers below so the scene visuals resync.

public TextEditCore Core { get; }

Property Value

TextEditCore

LastChangeReason

The reason of the most recent text change (valid inside/after OnTextChanged).

public TextChangeReason LastChangeReason { get; }

Property Value

TextChangeReason

LastEditWasDeletion

True when the most recent USER edit removed text (Backspace/Delete/word-delete/cut/clear) — the editable ComboBox restricts its search to exact matches on deletions so auto-complete cannot fight backspacing (ComboBox_Partial.cpp:4098–4106 keys this off VK_BACK).

public bool LastEditWasDeletion { get; }

Property Value

bool

FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
